text: I stand before a glass-walled room, observing the interplay of reflection and transparency. I see within a space made for collective inquiry, yet it is currently vacant. This emptiness heightens my awareness; I sense both anticipation and the uncanny stillness of preparation. Upon entering the exhibit, I am immersed in a world of innovation and memory. The words and technology surrounding me evoke a feeling of curious wonder—caught between nostalgia for human connection and the awe of creation. I feel like an observer on the threshold, pondering the ongoing dialogue between our inventions and our longing for meaning.
